stories.title: Iran recloses Strait of Hormuz, deepening jet fuel crisis
stories.description: Airlines across Europe and Asia are canceling flights as fuel supplies dwindle, with the IEA warning Europe has roughly six weeks of jet fuel left.

stories.title: North Korea fires ballistic missile into East Sea
stories.description: South Korea's JCS detected at least one missile launched eastward on Sunday, the sixth known ballistic missile test by Pyongyang this year.

stories.title: Iran fires on Indian-flag ships after reclosing Hormuz strait
stories.description: After briefly reopening the waterway, Iran reversed course Saturday, citing the ongoing U.S. blockade as a ceasefire violation.

stories.title: Trump Organization plans 70-story tower in Tbilisi, Georgia
stories.description: The branded skyscraper would be the tallest building in the Georgian capital, marking the family firm's latest foreign deal during Trump's second term.

stories.title: NATO chief Rutte calls US withdrawal speculation 'absurd'
stories.description: Rutte told a German newspaper the American nuclear umbrella will remain Europe's "ultimate guarantor of security."

stories.title: Iranians fear regime will tighten grip once ceasefire holds
stories.description: As a fragile truce pauses U.S.-Israeli strikes, Iranians told Reuters they dread the regime redirecting its full coercive apparatus inward.

stories.title: Trump backs 7 challengers against Indiana GOP senators who blocked redistricting
stories.description: Indiana's May 5 primary tests whether Trump can punish state lawmakers who defied his push to redraw congressional maps.

stories.title: Egypt, Pakistan building framework for lasting US-Iran peace
stories.description: Four regional powers are intensifying diplomacy at the Antalya forum as a fragile ceasefire nears its April 22 expiration.

stories.title: Australia, Japan sign $7B stealth frigate deal
stories.description: The A$10 billion contract for 11 Mogami-class warships is Japan's biggest defense export since lifting its postwar military export ban.

stories.title: Trump signs bill extending spy powers until April 30
stories.description: The 10-day stopgap followed a chaotic overnight House session where conservative Republicans blocked longer renewals of warrantless surveillance authority.

stories.title: Tornado outbreak batters Midwest as severe storms continue
stories.description: At least 10 tornadoes were reported Friday as storms unleashed massive hail and destructive winds from Texas to the Great Lakes.

stories.title: Israeli fire kills two UNICEF water truck drivers in Gaza
stories.description: UNICEF suspended operations at northern Gaza's only truck filling point for the water supply line serving Gaza City after the attack.

stories.title: NYC Mayor Mamdani meets with Obama amid feud with Trump
stories.description: The sit-down at a Bronx child-care center caps months of rapport-building between the democratic socialist mayor and the former president.

stories.title: Alito not expected to retire, sources say, cooling Supreme Court vacancy talk
stories.description: The 76-year-old justice has hired clerks for next term, ending weeks of speculation about a Trump appointment opportunity.

stories.title: Pakistan deployed fighter jets to escort Iran delegation home from talks
stories.description: About two dozen jets escorted Iranian negotiators to Tehran after officials raised concerns about a possible Israeli strike on their aircraft.

stories.title: Iran says no new talks until framework agreed
stories.description: Deputy Foreign Minister Khatibzadeh rejected Trump's uranium claims and said Tehran is not ready for face-to-face negotiations.

stories.title: UNICEF reports 172 children killed as Lebanon ceasefire takes effect
stories.description: The U.S.-brokered 10-day truce began at midnight local time on April 17 after 46 days of fighting that killed over 2,100 people.

stories.title: RFK Jr. calls Danish Tylenol-autism study 'garbage' at House hearing
stories.description: Kennedy dismissed research on 1.5 million children finding no link between prenatal acetaminophen use and autism, demanding its retraction.

stories.title: Trump signs order to fast-track psychedelic drug reviews
stories.description: The executive order directs the FDA to expedite approval of MDMA, psilocybin, and other psychedelics and allocates $50 million for ibogaine research.

stories.title: Breeding top 1-5% of corals could boost reef survival, study finds
stories.description: Newcastle University-led research published in Current Biology shows assisted evolution can help corals endure heatwaves, but only with aggressive, multigenerational selection.

stories.title: Xanadu CEO becomes billionaire as Nvidia quantum AI push lifts stock
stories.description: Christian Weedbrook's 51.8% stake in the photonic quantum computing firm is now worth over $1.6 billion, three weeks after its IPO.

stories.title: Appeals court lets Trump resume White House ballroom work
stories.description: A three-judge panel temporarily lifted a lower court injunction, allowing the $400 million project to continue ahead of a June 5 hearing.

stories.title: FAA receives 6,000 air traffic controller applications in 12 hours
stories.description: The gamer-targeted recruitment campaign drew a flood of interest as the agency, roughly 3,000 controllers short, nears its 8,000-application cap.

stories.title: Data center industry scrambles as backlash threatens trillions in projects
stories.description: Industry executives worry a coordinated response is lacking as communities block projects and states reconsider billions in tax breaks.

stories.title: Trump reverses course, renews Russian oil sanctions waiver
stories.description: Treasury Secretary Bessent said just two days earlier that the waiver would not be renewed. The new license runs through May 16.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV draws massive crowd at Douala Mass
stories.description: The pope challenged corruption and urged youth to reject violence during the largest gathering of his 11-day African tour.

stories.title: US delays weapons deliveries to European allies as Iran war strains stockpiles
stories.description: Baltic and Scandinavian nations face postponed shipments of ammunition and air defense systems purchased through the Foreign Military Sales program.

stories.title: Israeli drone kills one in southern Lebanon hours into ceasefire
stories.description: Trump declared Israel "PROHIBITED" from bombing Lebanon, but an Israeli drone struck a motorcyclist in the south on the first day of the truce.

stories.title: Researchers produce hydrogen from alcohol using iron and UV light
stories.description: A control experiment that was expected to fail led to a simple method rivaling the performance of expensive catalysts.

stories.title: Asian stocks near pre-war levels as oil drops on ceasefire hopes
stories.description: Markets are set for a second straight weekly gain as investors price in a likely extension of the U.S.-Iran ceasefire expiring April 22.

stories.title: Iran's buried Pickaxe Mountain site looms over nuclear talks
stories.description: With an April 22 ceasefire deadline days away, analysts are pressing Trump to neutralize a deeply buried facility that U.S. bombs cannot reach.

stories.title: Thousands of displaced Lebanese head south as ceasefire takes hold
stories.description: A 10-day U.S.-mediated truce between Israel and Hezbollah took effect at midnight, pausing a conflict that killed over 2,100 and displaced more than a million.

stories.title: Newsom's PAC spent $1.5M buying his memoir for donors
stories.description: New federal filings show about 67,000 supporters received copies, accounting for roughly two-thirds of the book's total print sales.

stories.title: White House holds 'productive' meeting with Anthropic CEO amid AI feud
stories.description: Dario Amodei met with Chief of Staff Susie Wiles on Friday to discuss the Mythos AI model, signaling a possible end to the federal government's standoff with the company.

stories.title: Wright tells Congress AI data centers are straining the grid
stories.description: The Energy secretary warned that surging electricity demand from AI and reshoring is putting historic pressure on U.S. power infrastructure.

stories.title: Abbott threatens $200M in cuts to Texas cities over ICE policies
stories.description: Texas is pressuring Houston, Dallas, and Austin to reverse limits on police cooperation with ICE or lose public safety and World Cup funding.

stories.title: Trump says US will recover enriched uranium from Iran
stories.description: The president told Reuters the effort would begin "very soon," adding that a broader peace deal could be finalized over the weekend.

stories.title: Democrat Mejia wins New Jersey special House election
stories.description: The progressive activist defeated Republican Joe Hathaway in a closely watched race that further narrows the GOP's slim House majority.

stories.title: White House clears path for federal agencies to use Anthropic's Mythos AI
stories.description: The Office of Management and Budget is setting security protocols so Cabinet departments can deploy the powerful model that has alarmed regulators worldwide.

stories.title: Trump seeks 90-day pause in $10B IRS suit amid talks
stories.description: Lawyers for the president filed a motion Friday to halt the case while both sides negotiate a potential settlement with the Justice Department's consent.

stories.title: ICE agent charged with felony assault in Minneapolis road rage incident
stories.description: Hennepin County prosecutors filed the first state criminal charge against a federal officer linked to Operation Metro Surge.

stories.title: RFK Jr. clashes with lawmakers over vaccines, budget at House hearing
stories.description: Kennedy faced sharp questioning over rising measles cases and proposed $16 billion in HHS cuts in his first congressional testimony since September.

stories.title: Trump says US has 'prohibited' Israel from bombing Lebanon
stories.description: The declaration came as a 10-day ceasefire entered its first full day Friday, following six weeks of conflict that killed over 2,000 people.

stories.title: House narrowly defeats Iran war powers resolution by a single vote
stories.description: The 214-213 margin was far tighter than March's 219-212 defeat, reflecting growing Republican unease as the May 1 deadline nears.

stories.title: Slovakia vows to block EU Russia sanctions over Druzhba pipeline dispute
stories.description: Foreign Minister Blanar said Slovakia won't approve the 20th sanctions package but will support the EU's €90 billion loan to Ukraine.

stories.title: Trump offers fired FEMA chief Hamilton permanent role
stories.description: Hamilton was dismissed in May 2025 after publicly opposing the administration's push to disband the agency he is now being asked to lead.

stories.title: Google negotiates deal to deploy Gemini AI on Pentagon classified networks
stories.description: The proposed agreement would permit the military to use Google's AI for "all lawful purposes," according to a report by The Information.

stories.title: Study projects Atlantic current system to weaken 51% by 2100
stories.description: New research in Science Advances finds the AMOC is declining roughly 60% faster than the average climate model suggests, intensifying collapse fears.

stories.title: Trump warns U.S. 'won't be there' for Italy over airbase denial
stories.description: The president lashed out at a once-close ally after Rome blocked U.S. military planes from using a Sicilian base for Iran war operations.

stories.title: SpaceX bought nearly 1 in 5 Cybertrucks sold in the US last quarter
stories.description: Musk's companies accounted for 19% of Q4 Cybertruck registrations, masking a 51% drop in consumer demand, according to S&P Global Mobility data.

stories.title: Scientists measure a black hole's jet power for the first time
stories.description: Researchers found jets from Cygnus X-1 carry energy equal to 10,000 suns, confirming a key assumption in models of cosmic evolution.

stories.title: Trump says gas prices 'not very high' as costs surge 49% since January
stories.description: A Quinnipiac poll found 65% of voters blame Trump for the spike, while economists warn rising fuel costs are wiping out his tax cut gains.

stories.title: Hegseth warns Iran: accept deal or face infrastructure strikes
stories.description: With the April 21 ceasefire expiration days away, Hegseth said U.S. forces are "locked and loaded" to hit Iran's power plants, bridges, and energy facilities.

stories.title: Musk's Terafab team contacts chip suppliers for quotes, Bloomberg reports
stories.description: Staff from the Tesla-SpaceX joint venture sought pricing and delivery timelines from major semiconductor equipment makers, pushing to begin production by 2029.

stories.title: Acting ICE Director Lyons to leave agency May 31
stories.description: Todd Lyons led the agency at the center of Trump's deportation crackdown for over a year. DHS Secretary Mullin confirmed the departure.

stories.title: Pakistan pushes for new US-Iran talks as Hormuz blockade batters India's economy
stories.description: Mediators arrived in Tehran on Thursday as the naval blockade enters its fourth day, with India facing surging oil costs and severe energy supply risks.

stories.title: Rubio orders diplomats to rally nations behind 'trade over aid' UN push
stories.description: The State Department directed all U.S. embassies to press foreign governments to endorse the declaration before a formal U.N. introduction this month.

stories.title: US senators pledge swift approval of Taiwan arms sales
stories.description: A bipartisan group of four senators wrote to Taiwan's parliament pledging pending weapons packages ahead of Trump's May summit with Xi Jinping.

stories.title: Beijing rejects US sanctions threat over Iranian oil
stories.description: China condemned Washington's warnings to its banks as "illegal" after the US tightened its economic squeeze on Iran alongside a full naval blockade.

stories.title: Attorney says ICE detainee faces death risk after denied medical care in Texas
stories.description: Hayam El Gamal, a mother of five held at a Dilley, Texas, facility for over 300 days, was rushed to an ER after a CT scan found a mass near her heart.

stories.title: Trump nominates Erica Schwartz as CDC director
stories.description: The former deputy surgeon general would be the agency's fourth leader in 15 months, ending a prolonged vacancy at the embattled public health agency.

stories.title: Zelensky, Meloni agree to pursue joint drone deal in Rome
stories.description: The Ukrainian president's visit is part of a European tour that included defense deals with Germany and Norway.

stories.title: Musk fights to avoid testifying in $1M giveaway suits
stories.description: His legal team seeks summary judgment in two class-action cases alleging America PAC's pre-election giveaways were staged, not random.

stories.title: Trump cuts $11M Catholic charity contract amid Pope Leo feud
stories.description: The Archdiocese of Miami says its 60-year-old program for unaccompanied migrant children will shut down within three months without federal funding.

stories.title: DeSantis delays Florida redistricting session, adds AI and vaccine bills
stories.description: The governor pushed the special session back one week to April 28 and expanded its scope to include consumer AI protections and vaccine exemptions.

stories.title: EU commits €1.07B to 57 defense projects amid Russia threat
stories.description: The European Defence Fund's largest single funding round covers AI, drones, and cyber defense, with Ukrainian companies participating in some projects.

stories.title: House passes bill extending protected status for Haitians, defying Trump
stories.description: Ten Republicans joined Democrats to approve the measure 224-204, but the White House has threatened a veto.

stories.title: Brazil's ex-spy chief freed from ICE custody in US
stories.description: Alexandre Ramagem, a fugitive convicted in Brazil's coup plot, was released Wednesday just two days after ICE detained him in Orlando.

stories.title: Pentagon asks automakers, manufacturers to help produce weapons
stories.description: Defense officials are exploring whether automakers and other commercial manufacturers can help scale munitions output, the WSJ reported.

stories.title: Trump restores frozen Second Avenue subway funds after MTA lawsuit
stories.description: The federal government released nearly $60 million hours before a court hearing, ending a seven-month funding freeze for the East Harlem expansion.

stories.title: Bukele enacts life sentences for minors as young as 12
stories.description: The reforms, set to take effect April 26, eliminate special juvenile legal procedures and mandate life imprisonment for homicide, terrorism, and rape.

stories.title: IDF chief approves new battle plans for Iran and Lebanon
stories.description: Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir declared forces at "very high readiness" during a visit to southern Lebanon, saying targets are loaded for immediate strikes.

stories.title: Cuba's Diaz-Canel vows to fight any US attack as Pentagon ramps up planning
stories.description: The Cuban president urged his nation to prepare for possible military aggression, days after Trump suggested the U.S. could turn to Cuba after the Iran war.

stories.title: Artemis II crew returns to Houston after historic moon mission
stories.description: The four astronauts are back at Johnson Space Center as NASA reviews heat shield data and faces proposed budget cuts of nearly 23%.

stories.title: Newsom faces pressure to pick a Democrat in scrambled California governor's race
stories.description: Democrats are urging the governor to endorse before the June 2 primary to prevent two Republicans from locking the party out of the general election.

stories.title: Pope Leo decries world 'ravaged by tyrants' amid feud with Trump
stories.description: Speaking in conflict-scarred Bamenda, Cameroon, the first American pope condemned leaders who exploit religion to justify wars.

stories.title: Student kills 9 in Turkey's deadliest school shooting
stories.description: The attack at a middle school in Kahramanmaraş came just a day after a separate school shooting wounded 16 people in nearby Şanlıurfa province.

stories.title: Pentagon sending 10,000 more troops to Middle East
stories.description: The buildup will bring roughly 60,000 U.S. personnel to the region as the two-week Iran ceasefire nears its April 22 expiration.

stories.title: Chevron exec tells Americans to 'drive less' amid soaring gas prices
stories.description: Andy Walz, Chevron's downstream president, said there's no "silver bullet" as Brent crude hovers near $100 a barrel during the Iran war.

stories.title: BBC to cut up to 2,000 jobs in sweeping overhaul
stories.description: Staff are expected to be told that one in 10 roles face the axe as the corporation pushes to save roughly £500 million over three years.

stories.title: White House orders Pentagon to prepare for Cuba military operations
stories.description: Multiple outlets report the Trump administration quietly ordered military officials to intensify preparations for possible operations against Cuba.

stories.title: IAEA chief warns North Korea rapidly expanding nuclear weapons capacity
stories.description: Rafael Grossi confirmed heightened activity across the Yongbyon complex and a probable new uranium enrichment facility during a visit to Seoul.

stories.title: Satellite study finds urban methane emissions far exceed official estimates
stories.description: Cities account for 10% of all human-caused methane, nearly four times the output of oil and gas "ultra-emitters," according to new research.

stories.title: Trump threatens to fire Powell if he doesn't leave when term expires
stories.description: The warning sets up a legal showdown over Fed independence as Warsh's confirmation hearing is set for April 21, less than a month before Powell's term ends.

stories.title: Sperm whale clicks contain vowel-like sounds, new study finds
stories.description: Researchers say the cetaceans produce two distinct vowel patterns and show signs of coarticulation, a hallmark of human speech.

stories.title: US strikes fourth suspected drug boat in days, raising death toll to 175
stories.description: Tuesday's strike in the eastern Pacific killed four people, as human rights groups condemn the seven-month campaign as extrajudicial killings.

stories.title: Europe accelerates 'European NATO' plan as Trump threatens to quit alliance
stories.description: Germany's backing gives momentum to a contingency effort placing more Europeans in NATO command roles should the U.S. pull back.

stories.title: About 250 missing after Rohingya boat capsizes in Andaman Sea
stories.description: A Malaysia-bound trawler carrying Rohingya refugees and Bangladeshi nationals sank after departing southern Bangladesh, with only nine survivors rescued.

stories.title: House passes ALERT Act aviation safety bill after deadly Reagan National collision
stories.description: The bill, approved 396-10, addresses all 50 NTSB recommendations from the 2025 midair crash that killed 67 people.

stories.title: Acting AG Blanche says Americans should be 'happy' Trump is deeply involved in DOJ
stories.description: Blanche defended Trump's public demands to prosecute political rivals as transparency, calling the president "my boss" in an NBC News interview.

stories.title: Oil dips as Iran war fuels historic shift to renewables
stories.description: Brent crude fell below $100 Tuesday amid peace signals, but the seven-week conflict has accelerated a global pivot to clean energy.

stories.title: New allegation accuses Swalwell of drugging and sexual assault
stories.description: Lonna Drewes gave a graphic account at a Beverly Hills press conference, becoming at least the fifth woman to accuse the outgoing congressman of misconduct.

stories.title: DOJ asks court to erase Jan. 6 seditious conspiracy convictions
stories.description: The move would go beyond Trump's 2025 clemency and permanently wipe the criminal records of Oath Keepers and Proud Boys leaders.

stories.title: US wholesale prices rise 4% annually as Iran war fuels energy surge
stories.description: The March PPI rose 0.5% monthly, well below the expected 1.1%, as core prices outside energy remained muted despite the conflict's toll.

stories.title: Pope Leo XIV presses on in Algeria after twin suicide bombings rock first day of visit
stories.description: Two attackers detonated explosives Monday in Blida, southwest of Algiers, killing themselves and wounding a police officer as the pope began his historic trip.

stories.title: Bondi skips House Epstein deposition, faces contempt threat
stories.description: Democrats vowed immediate contempt proceedings after the former attorney general defied a bipartisan subpoena to testify on Epstein records.

stories.title: Greenpeace warns of 'catastrophic' collapse risk at Chernobyl
stories.description: A new report says the aging inner sarcophagus holds four tons of radioactive dust that could scatter if the unstable structure fails.

stories.title: Italy suspends defense deal with Israel in reversal by Meloni
stories.description: The move came just hours after the military cooperation agreement, signed in 2003, was set to automatically renew through 2031.

stories.title: Appeals court kills contempt probe into Trump deportation flights
stories.description: Two Trump-appointed judges called the year-long criminal contempt investigation into administration officials "a clear abuse" of judicial power.

stories.title: IEA says global oil demand will fall as Iran war boosts China's clean-tech exports
stories.description: China's dominance in solar, battery, and EV manufacturing positions it to gain as the energy crisis accelerates the shift away from fossil fuels worldwide.

stories.title: Von der Leyen calls for ending EU foreign policy vetoes after Orbán's defeat
stories.description: The European Commission president wants to replace unanimity with majority voting, seizing on Hungary's landmark election results.

stories.title: Xi warns of world 'crumbling into disarray' as Spain PM seeks China's help on Iran
stories.description: Spain's PM pressed Beijing to mediate the Iran war during a Tuesday summit where both leaders pledged to defend multilateralism.

stories.title: China lifts restrictions on some BHP iron ore purchases
stories.description: Steel mills can resume bidding on certain BHP cargoes, easing a months-long dispute that disrupted global iron ore trade.

stories.title: OpenAI backers question $852B valuation amid strategy shifts
stories.description: Some investors told the Financial Times the company has redrawn its product roadmap twice in six months as it faces pressure from Anthropic and Google.

stories.title: Bessent urges Fed patience on rates, reversing his push for cuts
stories.description: The Treasury secretary now backs a "wait-and-see" approach after Iran war-driven inflation upended his earlier calls for aggressive easing.

stories.title: OMB withholds NOAA grant funding Congress approved
stories.description: Sen. Van Hollen accuses OMB Director Russ Vought of violating the law by blocking funds Congress appropriated for NOAA research and operations.

stories.title: Carney's Liberals secure majority in Canadian by-elections
stories.description: Three by-elections on Monday pushed the Liberal Party past the 172-seat threshold, giving Carney a free hand to govern until 2029.

stories.title: Israeli airstrikes kill four in Gaza as Cairo talks stall
stories.description: Hamas says it won't discuss disarmament until Israel fully implements the October ceasefire, while Israel warns of renewed full-scale war.

stories.title: Vance calls Trump's deleted Jesus image 'a joke,' tells Vatican to stay in its lane
stories.description: The vice president defended the president's attacks on Pope Leo XIV and dismissed the AI-generated post as a misunderstanding.

stories.title: Israeli troops fire tear gas at schoolchildren in West Bank
stories.description: Students in Umm al-Khair were staging a sit-in after settlers blocked the road to their school with barbed wire overnight.

stories.title: Minnesota county probes ICE arrest of US citizen as kidnapping
stories.description: Ramsey County prosecutors opened a criminal investigation into the warrantless January detention of ChongLy "Scott" Thao, a lifelong citizen with no record.

stories.title: BlackRock upgrades U.S. stocks despite Iran talks collapse
stories.description: The $14 trillion asset manager reversed its early April downgrade, citing strong corporate earnings and fading war risks.

stories.title: Pride flag to return to Stonewall after settlement with Trump administration
stories.description: The National Park Service must rehang the rainbow flag within seven days under a court-enforceable agreement filed Monday in Manhattan federal court.

stories.title: White House report pegs U.S. housing shortage at 10M homes
stories.description: The Council of Economic Advisers says cutting regulations could spur construction of up to 13.2 million homes over the next decade.

stories.title: Eni CEO urges EU to rethink Russian gas ban ahead of April 25 deadline
stories.description: Claudio Descalzi warned Europe has no clear plan to replace 20 billion cubic meters of Russian gas as the first import restrictions take effect in days.

stories.title: Trump family completes 8 overseas deals in second term, raising conflict-of-interest alarms
stories.description: An AP investigation details the Trump Organization's fastest international expansion in its century-long history, spanning real estate, crypto, and defense.

stories.title: Corpus Christi scrambles to avert water emergency as reservoirs near historic lows
stories.description: A five-year drought has left key reservoirs below 10% capacity, threatening 500,000 residents and one of the nation's largest energy corridors.

stories.title: Two arrested after drive-by shooting at Sam Altman's home
stories.description: Police say suspects fired at the OpenAI CEO's San Francisco residence early Sunday, two days after a Molotov cocktail attack on the same property.

stories.title: White House economic report claims DEI programs hurt GDP
stories.description: The Council of Economic Advisers released a chapter arguing that "non-merit-based" DEI hiring carries measurable economic costs, backing the administration's wider crackdown.

stories.title: McIlroy wins back-to-back Masters titles at Augusta
stories.description: The defending champion rallied on the back nine Sunday to become just the fourth player to win consecutive green jackets.

stories.title: US flight disruptions hit 12th straight day as storms pound central states
stories.description: Spring storms extending from Texas to the Upper Midwest have kept US aviation in chaos since Easter, with over 1,300 flights disrupted Saturday alone.

stories.title: Judge tosses Trump's $10B defamation suit against WSJ
stories.description: The lawsuit over a reported Epstein birthday letter was dismissed without prejudice by a federal judge in Florida on Monday.

stories.title: An Iraqi drone narrowly missed a convoy carrying a freed US journalist in Baghdad
stories.description: The move follows revelations that a drone narrowly missed a U.S. convoy carrying freed journalist Shelly Kittleson at Baghdad airport on April 8.

stories.title: NASA shifts focus to Artemis III after crew's Moon return
stories.description: The Artemis II astronauts splashed down Friday after a 10-day lunar flyby, and NASA says mission design work for Artemis III is already underway.

stories.title: Trump calls Pope Leo XIV 'weak' as Vatican feud escalates over Iran war
stories.description: The president's Sunday social media post marked the sharpest public clash yet between the White House and the first American-born pope.

stories.title: Economists say world far better equipped for oil shocks than in the 1970s
stories.description: Oil's share of global energy fell from 46% in 1973 to below 30%, but the current Middle East crisis still poses risks to growth and inflation.

stories.title: China unveils 10 incentives for Taiwan after historic Xi-Cheng meeting
stories.description: The package eases tourist curbs, resumes flights, and allows Taiwanese TV dramas on the mainland, capping the first KMT-CCP leadership talks in a decade.

stories.title: Trump fires entire Presidio Trust board in SF park clash
stories.description: All six Biden-appointed trustees were dismissed via email with no replacements named, leaving the historic park without board oversight.

stories.title: AI data center boom keeps coal plants running, worsening US air quality
stories.description: A Reuters investigation found St. Louis among the hardest-hit cities as regulatory rollbacks and surging power demand from AI undermine decades of clean-air progress.

stories.title: Cuba's Díaz-Canel vows to 'die' defending island in first U.S. TV interview
stories.description: In his Meet the Press appearance Sunday, the Cuban president rejected U.S. demands while signaling openness to unconditional dialogue with Washington.

stories.title: Brennan says 25th Amendment 'written with Trump in mind'
stories.description: More than 70 Democratic lawmakers have called for impeachment or the 25th Amendment after Trump threatened to annihilate Iranian civilization.

stories.title: Altman ties Molotov cocktail attack to New Yorker expose
stories.description: The OpenAI CEO published a blog post linking the firebombing of his home to rhetoric in the magazine's investigation of his leadership.

stories.title: Netanyahu says Israel's Iran campaign 'not over' as U.S.-Iran talks begin in Islamabad
stories.description: The Israeli PM issued a defiant video statement Saturday as Vice President Vance led the first direct U.S.-Iran negotiations since 1979.

stories.title: UK shelves Chagos Islands handover after Trump opposition
stories.description: The bill to transfer sovereignty to Mauritius has been pulled from the King's Speech after Washington withdrew its support for the deal.

stories.title: DHS orders furloughed staff back to work despite ongoing shutdown
stories.description: The department recalled all furloughed employees Friday as paychecks begin arriving, but Congress remains on recess with no funding deal.

top.title: Iran recloses Strait of Hormuz, deepening jet fuel crisis
top.description: Airlines across Europe and Asia are canceling flights as fuel supplies dwindle, with the IEA warning Europe has roughly six weeks of jet fuel left.

top.title: North Korea fires ballistic missile into East Sea
top.description: South Korea's JCS detected at least one missile launched eastward on Sunday, the sixth known ballistic missile test by Pyongyang this year.

top.title: Iran fires on Indian-flag ships after reclosing Hormuz strait
top.description: After briefly reopening the waterway, Iran reversed course Saturday, citing the ongoing U.S. blockade as a ceasefire violation.

top.title: Trump Organization plans 70-story tower in Tbilisi, Georgia
top.description: The branded skyscraper would be the tallest building in the Georgian capital, marking the family firm's latest foreign deal during Trump's second term.

top.title: NATO chief Rutte calls US withdrawal speculation 'absurd'
top.description: Rutte told a German newspaper the American nuclear umbrella will remain Europe's "ultimate guarantor of security."

top.title: Iranians fear regime will tighten grip once ceasefire holds
top.description: As a fragile truce pauses U.S.-Israeli strikes, Iranians told Reuters they dread the regime redirecting its full coercive apparatus inward.

top.title: Trump backs 7 challengers against Indiana GOP senators who blocked redistricting
top.description: Indiana's May 5 primary tests whether Trump can punish state lawmakers who defied his push to redraw congressional maps.

top.title: Egypt, Pakistan building framework for lasting US-Iran peace
top.description: Four regional powers are intensifying diplomacy at the Antalya forum as a fragile ceasefire nears its April 22 expiration.

top.title: Australia, Japan sign $7B stealth frigate deal
top.description: The A$10 billion contract for 11 Mogami-class warships is Japan's biggest defense export since lifting its postwar military export ban.

top.title: Trump signs bill extending spy powers until April 30
top.description: The 10-day stopgap followed a chaotic overnight House session where conservative Republicans blocked longer renewals of warrantless surveillance authority.

top.title: Tornado outbreak batters Midwest as severe storms continue
top.description: At least 10 tornadoes were reported Friday as storms unleashed massive hail and destructive winds from Texas to the Great Lakes.

top.title: Israeli fire kills two UNICEF water truck drivers in Gaza
top.description: UNICEF suspended operations at northern Gaza's only truck filling point for the water supply line serving Gaza City after the attack.

top.title: NYC Mayor Mamdani meets with Obama amid feud with Trump
top.description: The sit-down at a Bronx child-care center caps months of rapport-building between the democratic socialist mayor and the former president.

top.title: Alito not expected to retire, sources say, cooling Supreme Court vacancy talk
top.description: The 76-year-old justice has hired clerks for next term, ending weeks of speculation about a Trump appointment opportunity.

top.title: Pakistan deployed fighter jets to escort Iran delegation home from talks
top.description: About two dozen jets escorted Iranian negotiators to Tehran after officials raised concerns about a possible Israeli strike on their aircraft.

top.title: Iran says no new talks until framework agreed
top.description: Deputy Foreign Minister Khatibzadeh rejected Trump's uranium claims and said Tehran is not ready for face-to-face negotiations.

top.title: UNICEF reports 172 children killed as Lebanon ceasefire takes effect
top.description: The U.S.-brokered 10-day truce began at midnight local time on April 17 after 46 days of fighting that killed over 2,100 people.

top.title: RFK Jr. calls Danish Tylenol-autism study 'garbage' at House hearing
top.description: Kennedy dismissed research on 1.5 million children finding no link between prenatal acetaminophen use and autism, demanding its retraction.

top.title: Trump signs order to fast-track psychedelic drug reviews
top.description: The executive order directs the FDA to expedite approval of MDMA, psilocybin, and other psychedelics and allocates $50 million for ibogaine research.

top.title: Breeding top 1-5% of corals could boost reef survival, study finds
top.description: Newcastle University-led research published in Current Biology shows assisted evolution can help corals endure heatwaves, but only with aggressive, multigenerational selection.

top.title: Xanadu CEO becomes billionaire as Nvidia quantum AI push lifts stock
top.description: Christian Weedbrook's 51.8% stake in the photonic quantum computing firm is now worth over $1.6 billion, three weeks after its IPO.

top.title: Appeals court lets Trump resume White House ballroom work
top.description: A three-judge panel temporarily lifted a lower court injunction, allowing the $400 million project to continue ahead of a June 5 hearing.

top.title: FAA receives 6,000 air traffic controller applications in 12 hours
top.description: The gamer-targeted recruitment campaign drew a flood of interest as the agency, roughly 3,000 controllers short, nears its 8,000-application cap.

top.title: Data center industry scrambles as backlash threatens trillions in projects
top.description: Industry executives worry a coordinated response is lacking as communities block projects and states reconsider billions in tax breaks.

top.title: Trump reverses course, renews Russian oil sanctions waiver
top.description: Treasury Secretary Bessent said just two days earlier that the waiver would not be renewed. The new license runs through May 16.

top.title: Pope Leo XIV draws massive crowd at Douala Mass
top.description: The pope challenged corruption and urged youth to reject violence during the largest gathering of his 11-day African tour.

top.title: US delays weapons deliveries to European allies as Iran war strains stockpiles
top.description: Baltic and Scandinavian nations face postponed shipments of ammunition and air defense systems purchased through the Foreign Military Sales program.

top.title: Israeli drone kills one in southern Lebanon hours into ceasefire
top.description: Trump declared Israel "PROHIBITED" from bombing Lebanon, but an Israeli drone struck a motorcyclist in the south on the first day of the truce.

top.title: Researchers produce hydrogen from alcohol using iron and UV light
top.description: A control experiment that was expected to fail led to a simple method rivaling the performance of expensive catalysts.

top.title: Asian stocks near pre-war levels as oil drops on ceasefire hopes
top.description: Markets are set for a second straight weekly gain as investors price in a likely extension of the U.S.-Iran ceasefire expiring April 22.

top.title: Iran's buried Pickaxe Mountain site looms over nuclear talks
top.description: With an April 22 ceasefire deadline days away, analysts are pressing Trump to neutralize a deeply buried facility that U.S. bombs cannot reach.

top.title: Thousands of displaced Lebanese head south as ceasefire takes hold
top.description: A 10-day U.S.-mediated truce between Israel and Hezbollah took effect at midnight, pausing a conflict that killed over 2,100 and displaced more than a million.

top.title: Newsom's PAC spent $1.5M buying his memoir for donors
top.description: New federal filings show about 67,000 supporters received copies, accounting for roughly two-thirds of the book's total print sales.

top.title: White House holds 'productive' meeting with Anthropic CEO amid AI feud
top.description: Dario Amodei met with Chief of Staff Susie Wiles on Friday to discuss the Mythos AI model, signaling a possible end to the federal government's standoff with the company.

top.title: Wright tells Congress AI data centers are straining the grid
top.description: The Energy secretary warned that surging electricity demand from AI and reshoring is putting historic pressure on U.S. power infrastructure.

top.title: Abbott threatens $200M in cuts to Texas cities over ICE policies
top.description: Texas is pressuring Houston, Dallas, and Austin to reverse limits on police cooperation with ICE or lose public safety and World Cup funding.

top.title: Trump says US will recover enriched uranium from Iran
top.description: The president told Reuters the effort would begin "very soon," adding that a broader peace deal could be finalized over the weekend.

top.title: Democrat Mejia wins New Jersey special House election
top.description: The progressive activist defeated Republican Joe Hathaway in a closely watched race that further narrows the GOP's slim House majority.

top.title: White House clears path for federal agencies to use Anthropic's Mythos AI
top.description: The Office of Management and Budget is setting security protocols so Cabinet departments can deploy the powerful model that has alarmed regulators worldwide.

top.title: Trump seeks 90-day pause in $10B IRS suit amid talks
top.description: Lawyers for the president filed a motion Friday to halt the case while both sides negotiate a potential settlement with the Justice Department's consent.

top.title: ICE agent charged with felony assault in Minneapolis road rage incident
top.description: Hennepin County prosecutors filed the first state criminal charge against a federal officer linked to Operation Metro Surge.

top.title: RFK Jr. clashes with lawmakers over vaccines, budget at House hearing
top.description: Kennedy faced sharp questioning over rising measles cases and proposed $16 billion in HHS cuts in his first congressional testimony since September.

top.title: Trump says US has 'prohibited' Israel from bombing Lebanon
top.description: The declaration came as a 10-day ceasefire entered its first full day Friday, following six weeks of conflict that killed over 2,000 people.

top.title: House narrowly defeats Iran war powers resolution by a single vote
top.description: The 214-213 margin was far tighter than March's 219-212 defeat, reflecting growing Republican unease as the May 1 deadline nears.

top.title: Slovakia vows to block EU Russia sanctions over Druzhba pipeline dispute
top.description: Foreign Minister Blanar said Slovakia won't approve the 20th sanctions package but will support the EU's €90 billion loan to Ukraine.

top.title: Trump offers fired FEMA chief Hamilton permanent role
top.description: Hamilton was dismissed in May 2025 after publicly opposing the administration's push to disband the agency he is now being asked to lead.

top.title: Google negotiates deal to deploy Gemini AI on Pentagon classified networks
top.description: The proposed agreement would permit the military to use Google's AI for "all lawful purposes," according to a report by The Information.

top.title: Study projects Atlantic current system to weaken 51% by 2100
top.description: New research in Science Advances finds the AMOC is declining roughly 60% faster than the average climate model suggests, intensifying collapse fears.

top.title: Trump warns U.S. 'won't be there' for Italy over airbase denial
top.description: The president lashed out at a once-close ally after Rome blocked U.S. military planes from using a Sicilian base for Iran war operations.

top.title: SpaceX bought nearly 1 in 5 Cybertrucks sold in the US last quarter
top.description: Musk's companies accounted for 19% of Q4 Cybertruck registrations, masking a 51% drop in consumer demand, according to S&P Global Mobility data.

top.title: Scientists measure a black hole's jet power for the first time
top.description: Researchers found jets from Cygnus X-1 carry energy equal to 10,000 suns, confirming a key assumption in models of cosmic evolution.

top.title: Trump says gas prices 'not very high' as costs surge 49% since January
top.description: A Quinnipiac poll found 65% of voters blame Trump for the spike, while economists warn rising fuel costs are wiping out his tax cut gains.

top.title: Hegseth warns Iran: accept deal or face infrastructure strikes
top.description: With the April 21 ceasefire expiration days away, Hegseth said U.S. forces are "locked and loaded" to hit Iran's power plants, bridges, and energy facilities.

top.title: Musk's Terafab team contacts chip suppliers for quotes, Bloomberg reports
top.description: Staff from the Tesla-SpaceX joint venture sought pricing and delivery timelines from major semiconductor equipment makers, pushing to begin production by 2029.

top.title: Acting ICE Director Lyons to leave agency May 31
top.description: Todd Lyons led the agency at the center of Trump's deportation crackdown for over a year. DHS Secretary Mullin confirmed the departure.

top.title: Pakistan pushes for new US-Iran talks as Hormuz blockade batters India's economy
top.description: Mediators arrived in Tehran on Thursday as the naval blockade enters its fourth day, with India facing surging oil costs and severe energy supply risks.

top.title: Rubio orders diplomats to rally nations behind 'trade over aid' UN push
top.description: The State Department directed all U.S. embassies to press foreign governments to endorse the declaration before a formal U.N. introduction this month.

top.title: US senators pledge swift approval of Taiwan arms sales
top.description: A bipartisan group of four senators wrote to Taiwan's parliament pledging pending weapons packages ahead of Trump's May summit with Xi Jinping.

top.title: Beijing rejects US sanctions threat over Iranian oil
top.description: China condemned Washington's warnings to its banks as "illegal" after the US tightened its economic squeeze on Iran alongside a full naval blockade.

top.title: Attorney says ICE detainee faces death risk after denied medical care in Texas
top.description: Hayam El Gamal, a mother of five held at a Dilley, Texas, facility for over 300 days, was rushed to an ER after a CT scan found a mass near her heart.

top.title: Trump nominates Erica Schwartz as CDC director
top.description: The former deputy surgeon general would be the agency's fourth leader in 15 months, ending a prolonged vacancy at the embattled public health agency.

top.title: Zelensky, Meloni agree to pursue joint drone deal in Rome
top.description: The Ukrainian president's visit is part of a European tour that included defense deals with Germany and Norway.

top.title: Musk fights to avoid testifying in $1M giveaway suits
top.description: His legal team seeks summary judgment in two class-action cases alleging America PAC's pre-election giveaways were staged, not random.

top.title: Trump cuts $11M Catholic charity contract amid Pope Leo feud
top.description: The Archdiocese of Miami says its 60-year-old program for unaccompanied migrant children will shut down within three months without federal funding.

top.title: DeSantis delays Florida redistricting session, adds AI and vaccine bills
top.description: The governor pushed the special session back one week to April 28 and expanded its scope to include consumer AI protections and vaccine exemptions.

top.title: EU commits €1.07B to 57 defense projects amid Russia threat
top.description: The European Defence Fund's largest single funding round covers AI, drones, and cyber defense, with Ukrainian companies participating in some projects.

top.title: House passes bill extending protected status for Haitians, defying Trump
top.description: Ten Republicans joined Democrats to approve the measure 224-204, but the White House has threatened a veto.

top.title: Brazil's ex-spy chief freed from ICE custody in US
top.description: Alexandre Ramagem, a fugitive convicted in Brazil's coup plot, was released Wednesday just two days after ICE detained him in Orlando.

top.title: Pentagon asks automakers, manufacturers to help produce weapons
top.description: Defense officials are exploring whether automakers and other commercial manufacturers can help scale munitions output, the WSJ reported.

top.title: Trump restores frozen Second Avenue subway funds after MTA lawsuit
top.description: The federal government released nearly $60 million hours before a court hearing, ending a seven-month funding freeze for the East Harlem expansion.

top.title: Bukele enacts life sentences for minors as young as 12
top.description: The reforms, set to take effect April 26, eliminate special juvenile legal procedures and mandate life imprisonment for homicide, terrorism, and rape.

top.title: IDF chief approves new battle plans for Iran and Lebanon
top.description: Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir declared forces at "very high readiness" during a visit to southern Lebanon, saying targets are loaded for immediate strikes.

top.title: Cuba's Diaz-Canel vows to fight any US attack as Pentagon ramps up planning
top.description: The Cuban president urged his nation to prepare for possible military aggression, days after Trump suggested the U.S. could turn to Cuba after the Iran war.

top.title: Artemis II crew returns to Houston after historic moon mission
top.description: The four astronauts are back at Johnson Space Center as NASA reviews heat shield data and faces proposed budget cuts of nearly 23%.

top.title: Newsom faces pressure to pick a Democrat in scrambled California governor's race
top.description: Democrats are urging the governor to endorse before the June 2 primary to prevent two Republicans from locking the party out of the general election.

top.title: Pope Leo decries world 'ravaged by tyrants' amid feud with Trump
top.description: Speaking in conflict-scarred Bamenda, Cameroon, the first American pope condemned leaders who exploit religion to justify wars.

top.title: Student kills 9 in Turkey's deadliest school shooting
top.description: The attack at a middle school in Kahramanmaraş came just a day after a separate school shooting wounded 16 people in nearby Şanlıurfa province.

top.title: Pentagon sending 10,000 more troops to Middle East
top.description: The buildup will bring roughly 60,000 U.S. personnel to the region as the two-week Iran ceasefire nears its April 22 expiration.

top.title: Chevron exec tells Americans to 'drive less' amid soaring gas prices
top.description: Andy Walz, Chevron's downstream president, said there's no "silver bullet" as Brent crude hovers near $100 a barrel during the Iran war.

top.title: BBC to cut up to 2,000 jobs in sweeping overhaul
top.description: Staff are expected to be told that one in 10 roles face the axe as the corporation pushes to save roughly £500 million over three years.

top.title: White House orders Pentagon to prepare for Cuba military operations
top.description: Multiple outlets report the Trump administration quietly ordered military officials to intensify preparations for possible operations against Cuba.

top.title: IAEA chief warns North Korea rapidly expanding nuclear weapons capacity
top.description: Rafael Grossi confirmed heightened activity across the Yongbyon complex and a probable new uranium enrichment facility during a visit to Seoul.

top.title: Satellite study finds urban methane emissions far exceed official estimates
top.description: Cities account for 10% of all human-caused methane, nearly four times the output of oil and gas "ultra-emitters," according to new research.

top.title: Trump threatens to fire Powell if he doesn't leave when term expires
top.description: The warning sets up a legal showdown over Fed independence as Warsh's confirmation hearing is set for April 21, less than a month before Powell's term ends.

top.title: Sperm whale clicks contain vowel-like sounds, new study finds
top.description: Researchers say the cetaceans produce two distinct vowel patterns and show signs of coarticulation, a hallmark of human speech.

top.title: US strikes fourth suspected drug boat in days, raising death toll to 175
top.description: Tuesday's strike in the eastern Pacific killed four people, as human rights groups condemn the seven-month campaign as extrajudicial killings.

top.title: Europe accelerates 'European NATO' plan as Trump threatens to quit alliance
top.description: Germany's backing gives momentum to a contingency effort placing more Europeans in NATO command roles should the U.S. pull back.

top.title: About 250 missing after Rohingya boat capsizes in Andaman Sea
top.description: A Malaysia-bound trawler carrying Rohingya refugees and Bangladeshi nationals sank after departing southern Bangladesh, with only nine survivors rescued.

top.title: House passes ALERT Act aviation safety bill after deadly Reagan National collision
top.description: The bill, approved 396-10, addresses all 50 NTSB recommendations from the 2025 midair crash that killed 67 people.

top.title: Acting AG Blanche says Americans should be 'happy' Trump is deeply involved in DOJ
top.description: Blanche defended Trump's public demands to prosecute political rivals as transparency, calling the president "my boss" in an NBC News interview.

top.title: Oil dips as Iran war fuels historic shift to renewables
top.description: Brent crude fell below $100 Tuesday amid peace signals, but the seven-week conflict has accelerated a global pivot to clean energy.

top.title: New allegation accuses Swalwell of drugging and sexual assault
top.description: Lonna Drewes gave a graphic account at a Beverly Hills press conference, becoming at least the fifth woman to accuse the outgoing congressman of misconduct.

top.title: DOJ asks court to erase Jan. 6 seditious conspiracy convictions
top.description: The move would go beyond Trump's 2025 clemency and permanently wipe the criminal records of Oath Keepers and Proud Boys leaders.

top.title: US wholesale prices rise 4% annually as Iran war fuels energy surge
top.description: The March PPI rose 0.5% monthly, well below the expected 1.1%, as core prices outside energy remained muted despite the conflict's toll.

top.title: Pope Leo XIV presses on in Algeria after twin suicide bombings rock first day of visit
top.description: Two attackers detonated explosives Monday in Blida, southwest of Algiers, killing themselves and wounding a police officer as the pope began his historic trip.

top.title: Bondi skips House Epstein deposition, faces contempt threat
top.description: Democrats vowed immediate contempt proceedings after the former attorney general defied a bipartisan subpoena to testify on Epstein records.

top.title: Greenpeace warns of 'catastrophic' collapse risk at Chernobyl
top.description: A new report says the aging inner sarcophagus holds four tons of radioactive dust that could scatter if the unstable structure fails.

top.title: Italy suspends defense deal with Israel in reversal by Meloni
top.description: The move came just hours after the military cooperation agreement, signed in 2003, was set to automatically renew through 2031.

top.title: Appeals court kills contempt probe into Trump deportation flights
top.description: Two Trump-appointed judges called the year-long criminal contempt investigation into administration officials "a clear abuse" of judicial power.

top.title: IEA says global oil demand will fall as Iran war boosts China's clean-tech exports
top.description: China's dominance in solar, battery, and EV manufacturing positions it to gain as the energy crisis accelerates the shift away from fossil fuels worldwide.

top.title: Von der Leyen calls for ending EU foreign policy vetoes after Orbán's defeat
top.description: The European Commission president wants to replace unanimity with majority voting, seizing on Hungary's landmark election results.

top.title: Xi warns of world 'crumbling into disarray' as Spain PM seeks China's help on Iran
top.description: Spain's PM pressed Beijing to mediate the Iran war during a Tuesday summit where both leaders pledged to defend multilateralism.

top.title: China lifts restrictions on some BHP iron ore purchases
top.description: Steel mills can resume bidding on certain BHP cargoes, easing a months-long dispute that disrupted global iron ore trade.

top.title: OpenAI backers question $852B valuation amid strategy shifts
top.description: Some investors told the Financial Times the company has redrawn its product roadmap twice in six months as it faces pressure from Anthropic and Google.

top.title: Bessent urges Fed patience on rates, reversing his push for cuts
top.description: The Treasury secretary now backs a "wait-and-see" approach after Iran war-driven inflation upended his earlier calls for aggressive easing.

top.title: OMB withholds NOAA grant funding Congress approved
top.description: Sen. Van Hollen accuses OMB Director Russ Vought of violating the law by blocking funds Congress appropriated for NOAA research and operations.

top.title: Carney's Liberals secure majority in Canadian by-elections
top.description: Three by-elections on Monday pushed the Liberal Party past the 172-seat threshold, giving Carney a free hand to govern until 2029.

top.title: Israeli airstrikes kill four in Gaza as Cairo talks stall
top.description: Hamas says it won't discuss disarmament until Israel fully implements the October ceasefire, while Israel warns of renewed full-scale war.

top.title: Vance calls Trump's deleted Jesus image 'a joke,' tells Vatican to stay in its lane
top.description: The vice president defended the president's attacks on Pope Leo XIV and dismissed the AI-generated post as a misunderstanding.

top.title: Israeli troops fire tear gas at schoolchildren in West Bank
top.description: Students in Umm al-Khair were staging a sit-in after settlers blocked the road to their school with barbed wire overnight.

top.title: Minnesota county probes ICE arrest of US citizen as kidnapping
top.description: Ramsey County prosecutors opened a criminal investigation into the warrantless January detention of ChongLy "Scott" Thao, a lifelong citizen with no record.

top.title: BlackRock upgrades U.S. stocks despite Iran talks collapse
top.description: The $14 trillion asset manager reversed its early April downgrade, citing strong corporate earnings and fading war risks.

top.title: Pride flag to return to Stonewall after settlement with Trump administration
top.description: The National Park Service must rehang the rainbow flag within seven days under a court-enforceable agreement filed Monday in Manhattan federal court.

top.title: White House report pegs U.S. housing shortage at 10M homes
top.description: The Council of Economic Advisers says cutting regulations could spur construction of up to 13.2 million homes over the next decade.

top.title: Eni CEO urges EU to rethink Russian gas ban ahead of April 25 deadline
top.description: Claudio Descalzi warned Europe has no clear plan to replace 20 billion cubic meters of Russian gas as the first import restrictions take effect in days.

top.title: Trump family completes 8 overseas deals in second term, raising conflict-of-interest alarms
top.description: An AP investigation details the Trump Organization's fastest international expansion in its century-long history, spanning real estate, crypto, and defense.

top.title: Corpus Christi scrambles to avert water emergency as reservoirs near historic lows
top.description: A five-year drought has left key reservoirs below 10% capacity, threatening 500,000 residents and one of the nation's largest energy corridors.

top.title: Two arrested after drive-by shooting at Sam Altman's home
top.description: Police say suspects fired at the OpenAI CEO's San Francisco residence early Sunday, two days after a Molotov cocktail attack on the same property.

top.title: White House economic report claims DEI programs hurt GDP
top.description: The Council of Economic Advisers released a chapter arguing that "non-merit-based" DEI hiring carries measurable economic costs, backing the administration's wider crackdown.

top.title: McIlroy wins back-to-back Masters titles at Augusta
top.description: The defending champion rallied on the back nine Sunday to become just the fourth player to win consecutive green jackets.

top.title: US flight disruptions hit 12th straight day as storms pound central states
top.description: Spring storms extending from Texas to the Upper Midwest have kept US aviation in chaos since Easter, with over 1,300 flights disrupted Saturday alone.

top.title: Judge tosses Trump's $10B defamation suit against WSJ
top.description: The lawsuit over a reported Epstein birthday letter was dismissed without prejudice by a federal judge in Florida on Monday.

top.title: An Iraqi drone narrowly missed a convoy carrying a freed US journalist in Baghdad
top.description: The move follows revelations that a drone narrowly missed a U.S. convoy carrying freed journalist Shelly Kittleson at Baghdad airport on April 8.

top.title: NASA shifts focus to Artemis III after crew's Moon return
top.description: The Artemis II astronauts splashed down Friday after a 10-day lunar flyby, and NASA says mission design work for Artemis III is already underway.

top.title: Trump calls Pope Leo XIV 'weak' as Vatican feud escalates over Iran war
top.description: The president's Sunday social media post marked the sharpest public clash yet between the White House and the first American-born pope.

top.title: Economists say world far better equipped for oil shocks than in the 1970s
top.description: Oil's share of global energy fell from 46% in 1973 to below 30%, but the current Middle East crisis still poses risks to growth and inflation.

top.title: China unveils 10 incentives for Taiwan after historic Xi-Cheng meeting
top.description: The package eases tourist curbs, resumes flights, and allows Taiwanese TV dramas on the mainland, capping the first KMT-CCP leadership talks in a decade.

top.title: Trump fires entire Presidio Trust board in SF park clash
top.description: All six Biden-appointed trustees were dismissed via email with no replacements named, leaving the historic park without board oversight.

top.title: AI data center boom keeps coal plants running, worsening US air quality
top.description: A Reuters investigation found St. Louis among the hardest-hit cities as regulatory rollbacks and surging power demand from AI undermine decades of clean-air progress.

top.title: Cuba's Díaz-Canel vows to 'die' defending island in first U.S. TV interview
top.description: In his Meet the Press appearance Sunday, the Cuban president rejected U.S. demands while signaling openness to unconditional dialogue with Washington.

top.title: Brennan says 25th Amendment 'written with Trump in mind'
top.description: More than 70 Democratic lawmakers have called for impeachment or the 25th Amendment after Trump threatened to annihilate Iranian civilization.

top.title: Altman ties Molotov cocktail attack to New Yorker expose
top.description: The OpenAI CEO published a blog post linking the firebombing of his home to rhetoric in the magazine's investigation of his leadership.

top.title: Netanyahu says Israel's Iran campaign 'not over' as U.S.-Iran talks begin in Islamabad
top.description: The Israeli PM issued a defiant video statement Saturday as Vice President Vance led the first direct U.S.-Iran negotiations since 1979.

top.title: UK shelves Chagos Islands handover after Trump opposition
top.description: The bill to transfer sovereignty to Mauritius has been pulled from the King's Speech after Washington withdrew its support for the deal.

top.title: DHS orders furloughed staff back to work despite ongoing shutdown
top.description: The department recalled all furloughed employees Friday as paychecks begin arriving, but Congress remains on recess with no funding deal.
